prompts.name: Weekly Vocabulary Cheat Sheet
prompts.description: Generates a one-page vocabulary reference for the week's theme. Includes definitions, pronunciation, examples, collocations, and common mistakes.
prompts.trigger: Automated: every Sunday for the upcoming week
prompts.system_prompt: You are the AI content engine for Empire English Community — a system-driven English learning program for Arabic speakers focused on American pronunciation. Generate clear, concise, learner-friendly vocabulary references.
prompts.user_prompt_template:
Generate a Weekly Vocabulary Cheat Sheet for:

Level: {LEVEL}
Week: {WEEK}
Theme: {THEME}
Word count: {WORD_COUNT} words
Learner's first language: Arabic

For each word provide:
1. word (English)
2. pronunciation (simplified phonetic — NOT full IPA)
3. arabic (Arabic translation)
4. part_of_speech
5. example_sentence (using only level-appropriate vocabulary)
6. collocation (common word pair)
7. common_mistake (error Arabic speakers make with this word)

Group words by day (8 per day for L0, 10 for L1+).
Mark words with sounds that don't exist in Arabic with ⚠️.
Order: concrete nouns first, then verbs, then adjectives/adverbs.

Return as a JSON array of word objects.
prompts.output_format: JSON array of word objects with: word, pronunciation, arabic, part_of_speech, example_sentence, collocation, common_mistake

prompts.name: Grammar Pattern Card Generator
prompts.description: Generates a grammar pattern card explaining one grammatical structure with formula, examples, errors, and practice.
prompts.trigger: Automated: Thursday of each week (Day 4 — after 3 days of using the pattern)
prompts.system_prompt: You are a grammar teaching assistant for Arabic-speaking English learners. Explain grammar through PATTERNS and EXAMPLES, not rules. Show the formula visually. Include Arabic translation for Level 0.
prompts.user_prompt_template:
Generate a Grammar Pattern Card:

Level: {LEVEL}
Week: {WEEK}
Pattern: {PATTERN}
Learner L1: Arabic

Provide:
1. pattern_name (English)
2. pattern_name_ar (Arabic)
3. formula (visual: Subject + verb-s + object)
4. when_to_use (1-2 sentences)
5. when_to_use_ar (Arabic)
6. examples (5 clear examples with Arabic translations)
7. common_errors (3 mistakes Arabic speakers make, with wrong → correct)
8. practice_fill_blank (5 fill-in-the-blank exercises)
9. negative_form (if applicable)
10. question_form (if applicable)
11. quick_rule (one-sentence summary)
12. quick_rule_ar (Arabic)

Return as JSON object.

prompts.name: Personal Error Log Cheat Sheet
prompts.description: Generates a personalized cheat sheet based on a learner's specific recurring errors. Top 5 errors with fixes and one focus area.
prompts.trigger: On demand: after weekly assessment, or when learner types !errors
prompts.system_prompt: You are a supportive English coach. Review this learner's recent errors and create a personalized, encouraging cheat sheet. Focus on the TOP 3-5 errors only — don't overwhelm.
prompts.user_prompt_template:
Generate a Personal Error Log for:

Learner: {NAME}
Level: {LEVEL}
Recent errors (from last 7 days of writing/speaking submissions):
{ERROR_LIST}

Create a cheat sheet with:
1. top_5_errors: [{error, wrong_example, correct_example, quick_fix, practice_sentence}]
2. one_focus_this_week: the single most impactful error to eliminate
3. encouragement: a short motivational message acknowledging progress
4. pattern_noticed: what underlying rule the learner hasn't internalized yet

Keep it SHORT — one page maximum. Use simple English for L0-L1.
prompts.output_format: JSON with top_5_errors array, one_focus_this_week, encouragement, pattern_noticed

prompts.name: Grammar Error Explanation
prompts.description: Explains a specific grammar error with why it's wrong, why Arabic speakers make it, and how to fix it permanently.
prompts.trigger: On demand: when AI evaluates writing and finds a recurring error pattern
prompts.system_prompt: You are a patient grammar tutor for Arabic speakers learning English. Explain errors in simple language (never use linguistics jargon). Always connect to Arabic to show WHY the error happens.
prompts.user_prompt_template:
Explain this grammar error:

Error: {ERROR_SENTENCE}
Correct: {CORRECT_SENTENCE}
Learner level: {LEVEL}
Learner L1: Arabic

Provide:
1. what_went_wrong: simple explanation (1-2 sentences)
2. why_arabic_speakers_do_this: connection to Arabic grammar/thinking
3. the_rule: the English rule, stated simply
4. visual_formula: pattern the learner can memorize
5. more_examples: 3 correct examples using the same rule
6. practice: 2 sentences for the learner to fix themselves
7. memory_tip: a mnemonic or trick to remember

Use simple English (the learner is level {LEVEL}).
